data.title: Monolithic Structure
data.description: Google's core architecture relies heavily on a monolithic structure that centralizes control, enabling robust data management.
data.technical_spec: Built using a globally distributed monolithic database system called Spanner, which backs many Google services.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Effective load balancing is crucial for Google's ability to handle millions of queries per second.
data.technical_spec: Uses proprietary algorithms for distributing queries across global data centers to optimize speed and reliability.

data.title: Microservices and Containers
data.description: Gradual integration of microservices to increase development velocity and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Implemented through Borg and Kubernetes, orchestration and management tools for deploying containers at scale.

data.title: Data Replication
data.description: Ensures data redundancy and reliability through real-time data replication capabilities.
data.technical_spec: Employs systems like Colossus, the next-generation distributed file system for cross-data center redundancy.

data.title: Edge Computing Infrastructure
data.description: Enhances user experience by processing data closer to the user's location.
data.technical_spec: Implements edge nodes and data centers positioned strategically for minimizing latency.
